events.title: Bus blast in Colombia kills 13 amid rising violence
events.description: An explosive device killed 13 people traveling on a bus in southwestern Colombia on Saturday, an attack the country's army chief described as a “terrorist act" that also left at least 38 injured as violence linked to drug trafficking in the region escalates. FRANCE 24's Léa Hurel reports.
events.ticker_label: 💥 Bus blast in Colombia kills 13 amid rising violence

events.title: Mali: Explosions, gunfire rock main military camp near capital
events.description: Mali's army said unidentified "terrorist" groups had attacked several positions in the capital and elsewhere in the country.
events.ticker_label: 💥 Mali: Explosions, gunfire rock main military camp near capital

events.title: Ukraine marks 40th anniversary of the Chernobyl nuclear plant disaster
events.description: Ukraine on Sunday marks the 40th anniversary of the explosion at the Chernobyl nuclear power plant – the worst civilian nuclear disaster in history. It comes four years into the Russian invasion that has put the plant once again under threat and raised risks of another radioactive catastrophe.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Ukraine marks 40th anniversary of the Chernobyl nuclear plant disaster

events.title: Heatwaves, floods and wildfires pose rising threat to democracy, report finds
events.description: Research shows natural hazards linked to climate crisis disrupted 23 elections in 18 countries in 2024 Democracy is under mounting threat from the climate crisis, with new analysis documenting how elections are increasingly shaped not only by political forces but also by floods, wildfires and extreme weather. At least 94 elections and referendums across 52 countries have been disrupted by climate-related impacts over the last two decades, researchers found. Continue reading...
events.ticker_label: 🌪️ Heatwaves, floods and wildfires pose rising threat to democracy, report finds

events.title: Two-thirds of global hunger concentrated in 10 conflict-hit countries
events.description: A growing share of global hunger is becoming entrenched in a small group of conflict-hit countries, with two-thirds of people facing acute food insecurity concentrated in just 10 nations, a major international report backed by UN agencies warns.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Two-thirds of global hunger concentrated in 10 conflict-hit countries

events.title: AP Was There: Early Chernobyl victims buried in Moscow cemetery
events.description: In the weeks after the April 26, 1986, explosion and fire at the Chernobyl nuclear power plant, it was difficult to get any information about the scope of the disaster, aside from terse announcements from the government of the Soviet Union
events.ticker_label: 💥 AP Was There: Early Chernobyl victims buried in Moscow cemetery

events.title: Hamas influence looms over Gaza elections as experts warn vote could backfire
events.description: Hamas reportedly increased its hold in Gaza as Deir al-Balah holds its first elections in 20 years, raising concerns about terrorist group influence.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Hamas influence looms over Gaza elections as experts warn vote could backfire

events.title: Militants and separatists launch coordinated attacks across Mali
events.description: Al-Qaida-linked group JNIM claims responsibility for strikes on airport in capital, Bamako and four other cities Islamic militants and separatists attacked several locations in Mali’s capital and other cities on Saturday in one of the largest coordinated attacks in the country in recent years. The al-Qaida-linked militant group JNIM claimed responsibility for the attacks on Bamako’s international airport and four other cities in central and northern Mali on its website, Az-Zallaqa. It said the attacks were carried out jointly with the Azawad Liberation Front, a Tuareg-led separatist group. Continue reading...
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Militants and separatists launch coordinated attacks across Mali

events.title: Deadly Israeli strikes hit Lebanon despite ceasefire extension
events.description: Lebanese state media reported a series of new Israeli strikes in at least four different locations in the country's south late Saturday, after Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu ordered his military to attack Hezbollah following alleged ceasefire breaches. Attacks earlier in the day reportedly killed six people, despite a recently extended ceasefire.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Deadly Israeli strikes hit Lebanon despite ceasefire extension

events.title: Terrorists launch simultaneous attacks in Mali, authorities say
events.description: Terrorists have carried out attacks in various locations across Mali, according to the country's army.
events.ticker_label: 💥 Terrorists launch simultaneous attacks in Mali, authorities say

events.title: Rising nuclear risks put global security pact to the test at UN Headquarters
events.description: At a moment of mounting nuclear danger and deepening global mistrust, governments from nearly every country will convene at the United Nations next week to confront a central question: will the world’s main barrier against the spread of nuclear weapons still hold?

events.title: World News in Brief: Airliner safety warning, drought in Somalia, solar boosts farming in Ethiopia
events.description: The head of the UN’s civil aviation agency, ICAO, has called for immediate global action to protect commercial flights from escalating military threats, including missiles, drones, and GPS signal jamming.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 World News in Brief: Airliner safety warning, drought in Somalia, solar boosts farming in Ethiopia

events.title: US military kills two suspected narco-terrorists in strike on drug-trafficking vessel in the Pacific
events.description: U.S. Southern Command says a lethal strike on a suspected narco-trafficking vessel in the Eastern Pacific killed two suspected narco-terrorists.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 US military kills two suspected narco-terrorists in strike on drug-trafficking vessel in the Pacific

events.title: 40 years since Chernobyl disaster: Soviet Union's 'top-down culture' partly to blame
events.description: Ukraine marked the 40th anniversary of the Chernobyl nuclear disaster on Sunday. FRANCE 24 spoke with Michael Bluck, Director of the Centre for Nuclear Engineering at Imperial College London, to look back at the tragic event that marked history. Pointing to various factors that caused the explosion at Chernobyl, Bluck said that the Soviet Union's culting of top-down rule was also at blame.

events.title: Russian attacks kill 4 and wound more than 20 in Ukraine's Dnipro; 1 killed in Russia
events.description: Russian drone and missile strikes on the Ukrainian city of Dnipro killed at least four people and wounded 21 overnight, local authorities said Saturday
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Russian attacks kill 4 and wound more than 20 in Ukraine's Dnipro; 1 killed in Russia

events.title: World leaders condemn ‘unacceptable’ violence after armed attack disrupts WH Correspondents’ Dinner
events.description: Global leaders expressed relief that Trump was unharmed after an armed suspect allegedly attacked the White House Correspondents' Dinner in Washington.

events.title: 40 years since the world’s worst nuclear disaster in Ukraine
events.description: 40 years since the world’s worst nuclear disaster in Ukraine

events.title: US military kills two more people in strike on alleged drug boat in Pacific
events.description: Small boat destroyed in video posted on social media as US campaign has killed at least 178 people since September The US military announced on Friday that it killed two people in an attack on a boat in the eastern Pacific, part of a series of deadly strikes on vessels in recent months which it claims are targeting “narco-trafficking” operations. The US Southern Command declared in a social media post on X that Gen Francis L Donovan directed Joint Task Force Southern Spear, the counter-narcotics unit that operates in the region, to carry out a lethal strike. The US military posted a video, which it labeled unclassified, showing a small boat being destroyed in an explosion. Continue reading...
events.ticker_label: 🪖 US military kills two more people in strike on alleged drug boat in Pacific

events.title: Despite Russia’s war, one Ukrainian city still gathers for midnight Chernobyl vigil
events.description: Ukrainians gathered in the city of Slavutych to remember those affected by the Chernobyl nuclear disaster 40 years ago
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Despite Russia’s war, one Ukrainian city still gathers for midnight Chernobyl vigil

events.title: Middle East war: After oil and gas, concerns grow over minerals crunch
events.description: The shipping crisis in the Strait of Hormuz caused by war in the Middle East has exposed a new threat: a looming shortage of strategic minerals that drive economies all over the world – and a race by countries to obtain them.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Middle East war: After oil and gas, concerns grow over minerals crunch

events.title: Armed groups claim attacks across Mali
events.description: An Al-Qaeda-linked jihadist group said Saturday that its fighters had joined forces with Tuareg rebels to launch attacks against the army across junta-ruled Mali. Helicopters buzzed over Bamako and around the international airport and fighting was reported at a nearby military base in what was one of the most complex attacks in years in the impoverished west African country.

events.title: Mali hit by wave of coordinated attacks from armed groups
events.description: Gunfire and explosions have rocked Mali's capital Bamako and other key cities in one of the most significant coordinated attacks in years, as armed groups, including jihadist insurgents and separatist rebels exploit worsening insecurity in the Sahel region.

events.title: Japan: Raging wildfires cause mass evacuations
events.description: More than 1,000 firefighters are battling two blazes in northern Japan, with some 3,000 people urged to leave their homes. The fires come as the country's winters become increasingly dry.
events.ticker_label: 🌪️ Japan: Raging wildfires cause mass evacuations

events.title: World News in Brief: Civilians at risk in Gaza and West Bank, Duterte to go on trial in ICC, Yemen detainees update
events.description: Continued strikes, shelling and gunfire in residential areas of Gaza are raising alarm over civilian safety, the UN relief coordination office OCHA has warned.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 World News in Brief: Civilians at risk in Gaza and West Bank, Duterte to go on trial in ICC, Yemen detainees update

events.title: Altman apologizes after OpenAI failed to alert police before fatal Canada shooting
events.description: OpenAI said the company had identified an account using abuse-detection efforts, but determined at the time it didn’t meet threshold for legal referral The head of OpenAI has written a letter apologizing that his company didn’t alert law enforcement about the online behavior of a person who shot and killed eight people in Tumbler Ridge, British Columbia. In the letter posted on Friday, Sam Altman expressed his deepest condolences to the entire community. Continue reading...
events.ticker_label: 💥 Altman apologizes after OpenAI failed to alert police before fatal Canada shooting

events.title: Malian Defence Minister Sadio Camara killed by rebels
events.description: Mali’s Defence Minister Sadio Camara was killed in coordinated attacks targeting military sites, officials said, marking a major blow to the ruling junta. The killing comes amid a surge in violence by jihadist and rebel groups, raising fresh concerns over security in the West African nation. FRANCE 24's Justice Baidoo reports

events.title: SECURITY COUNCIL LIVE: Regional war threatens Syria’s fragile transition
events.description: The Security Council is meeting now at a critical juncture for Syria’s fragile transition. While the Iran-US ceasefire has temporarily eased regional spillover from Lebanon and elsewhere, conditions remain volatile in Syria. The reported US military withdrawal now completed, marks a milestone, yet the chamber faces a country grappling with deep internal fissures and external pressures. Follow full meetings coverage below and UN News app users should click here.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 SECURITY COUNCIL LIVE: Regional war threatens Syria’s fragile transition

events.title: Despite ceasefire, Hormuz tensions continue to throttle supply chains worldwide
events.description: A temporary ceasefire between the United States and Iran has been extended, offering an opening for diplomacy – but persistent tensions in the Strait of Hormuz are compounding trade disruptions and intensifying pressure on humanitarian operations and vulnerable communities far beyond the Gulf.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Despite ceasefire, Hormuz tensions continue to throttle supply chains worldwide

events.title: SECURITY COUNCIL LIVE: Update on Haiti as humanitarian crisis continues alongside gang violence
events.description: The Security Council is addressing the deepening crisis in Haiti on Thursday, centered on the Secretary-General’s latest report which highlights a security landscape of both intensified enforcement and rising civilian risk. While operations including by the UN-backed Gang Suppression Force (GSF) between December and February resulted in the deaths of 1,343 suspected gang members, the humanitarian cost remains staggering. The UN Special Representative told ambassadors elections are the “only legitimate path” back to political stability, while his counterpart with the GSF said its success depends on “effective coordination”. Follow full meetings coverage below.
events.ticker_label: 💥 SECURITY COUNCIL LIVE: Update on Haiti as humanitarian crisis continues alongside gang violence

events.title: Netanyahu orders army to 'vigorously attack' Hezbollah in Lebanon
events.description: The Israeli PM's directive comes as six people were killed by strikes in Lebanon despite a ceasefire being extended by three weeks.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Netanyahu orders army to 'vigorously attack' Hezbollah in Lebanon

events.title: Thousands at risk after multi-million dollar Everest flood warning system left to rust
events.description: The flood warning system at Imja glacial lake has not been maintained since 2016, fearful locals tell BBC.
events.ticker_label: 🌪️ Thousands at risk after multi-million dollar Everest flood warning system left to rust

events.title: Mexico says 2 US federal agents who died were not authorized to participate in any local operation

events.ticker_label: 💥 Mexico says 2 US federal agents who died were not authorized to participate in any local operation

events.title: Patient allegedly attacks several nurses, police and member of the public at Sydney hospital
events.description: Calls for more security measures after man, 51, arrested over alleged assaults at Prince of Wales hospital Follow our Australia news live blog for latest updates Get our breaking news email , free app or daily news podcast A hospital attack that left a nurse seriously injured has fuelled calls for improved safety and security after several violent incidents in emergency departments. Police restrained a 51-year-old patient after he allegedly assaulted several nurses, police and a member of the public in a Saturday night melee at Sydney’s Prince of Wales hospital. Continue reading...

events.title: US embassy in London warns to 'exercise increased caution' near Jewish sites in UK, Europe after attacks
events.description: Americans in the U.K. and Europe are urged to exercise caution after the U.S. Embassy in London cited rising threats to Jewish and American areas.

events.title: Nuclear experts warn Iran’s uranium ‘right’ is a myth, say Trump is right to hold firm

events.ticker_label: 🪖 Nuclear experts warn Iran’s uranium ‘right’ is a myth, say Trump is right to hold firm

events.title: Key suspect in notorious Tadamon massacre during Syria civil war arrested
events.description: Amjad Youssef was wanted over the mass killing of blindfolded civilians in April 2013 in the district of Damascus.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Key suspect in notorious Tadamon massacre during Syria civil war arrested

events.title: UK position on Falklands will not change, No 10 says after leaked Pentagon memo
events.description: Internal email proposes US should reassess support for UK claim to islands because of lack of support for Iran war UK politics live – latest updates Downing Street has been forced to insist that Britain will not yield sovereignty over the Falkland Islands, after a leaked Pentagon email proposed the US should reassess its support for the UK’s claim on the islands because of a lack of British support over Iran. The memo reflected ways in which the Trump administration could punish Britain for failing to follow the US lead in bombing Iran, and comes before a potentially fraught three-day state visit to the US by King Charles. Continue reading...
events.ticker_label: 🪖 UK position on Falklands will not change, No 10 says after leaked Pentagon memo

events.title: Dad of illegal immigrant DUI victim issues warning to Americans over lax driver's license laws
events.description: Joe Abraham warns Americans after his daughter was killed by an illegal immigrant drunken driver, urging stricter driver's license laws and proper vetting.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Dad of illegal immigrant DUI victim issues warning to Americans over lax driver's license laws

events.title: Zelenskyy ready to hold Ukraine-Russia talks in Azerbaijan
events.description: Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelenskyy said his country is ready to hold talks with Russia in Azerbaijan. Meanwhile, Russian attacks killed several people across Ukraine.

events.description: Authorities in southwest Colombia are on high alert following a spate of explosive attacks and thwarted attempts to target public infrastructure

events.title: What counts as the woods? Judge axes Nova Scotia’s ban that defied ‘commonsense definitions’
events.description: The court sided with a Canadian hiker who deliberately challenged the order imposed to curb spread of wildfires As wildfires raged across Nova Scotia last summer, the Canadian province made a simple plea to residents: stay away from the woods. As the situation deteriorated, authorities turned the request into a prohibition : anyone caught hiking under the shade of the forest canopy faced a C$25,000 fine – a figure more than half the average worker’s yearly salary. Continue reading...
events.ticker_label: 🌪️ What counts as the woods? Judge axes Nova Scotia’s ban that defied ‘commonsense definitions’

events.title: Florida officials investigate planned ‘Sloth World’ attraction after 31 sloths die in warehouse
events.description: Languorous tree dwellers from Guyana and Peru died from ‘cold stun’ in warehouse with no power or running water Sign up for the Breaking News US newsletter email Wildlife officials in Florida said in a newly released report that dozens of sloths taken from South American rainforests for display at a controversial new tourist attraction in Orlando died in the care of their new owners. An incident report from the Florida fish and wildlife conservation commission (FWC) said that 31 of the mammals procured from Peru and Guyana by the owners of a forthcoming attraction called Sloth World perished in a storage warehouse more than a year ago, between December 2024 and February 2025. Continue reading...
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Florida officials investigate planned ‘Sloth World’ attraction after 31 sloths die in warehouse

events.title: Police in Northern Ireland declare security alert after reports of a car bomb explosion

events.ticker_label: 💥 Police in Northern Ireland declare security alert after reports of a car bomb explosion

events.title: Libya: UN mission chief laments lack of progress towards national renewal

events.title: Iran’s good cop, bad cop game implodes as experts warn regime views US as 'evil'

events.ticker_label: 🪖 Iran’s good cop, bad cop game implodes as experts warn regime views US as 'evil'

events.title: ‘Look, no hands’: China chases the driverless dream at Beijing car show
events.description: As domestic sales slow, manufacturers are investing in AI and seeking growth in technology and in overseas markets At the world’s biggest car fair, which opened in Beijing on Friday, there were hundreds of manufacturers, more than 1,000 vehicles, hundreds of thousands of enthusiasts – and hardly anyone behind a wheel. China’s car companies have cornered the domestic electric vehicle market, and are increasingly visible on the global stage . Now they are turning their attention to what they are betting is the future of mobility: autonomous driving. Continue reading...

events.title: Cuban ambassador says U.S. is to blame for island's crisis
events.description: Even as the U.S. threatens military action, a senior Cuban ambassador blames Washington for the country's crisis and says Cuba is prepared for confrontation if diplomacy collapses.

events.title: Is Germany's pension system still secure?
events.description: Chancellor Friedrich Merz has sparked controversy by describing Germany's state pension as no more than "basic cover." What did he mean by that? And how does Germany compare internationally?
events.ticker_label: 💥 Is Germany's pension system still secure?

events.title: 40 years after Chernobyl: Pripyat today
events.description: After the catastrophic accident in the nearby nuclear reactor, the city of Pripyat had to be completely evacuated. Some 50,000 people left their homes forever. DW visited the town with a former resident 40 years later.

events.title: Mexico says alleged CIA agents 'not authorized' to operate on its soil
events.description: Two US citizens believed to be CIA agents were killed in a car accident following an anti-drug trafficking operation. Mexico says they entered the country as a tourist and a diplomat.

events.title: 🔴 Mali Defence Minister Sadio Camara killed in al Qaeda‑linked attack near Bamako, RFI reports
events.description: No description available.

events.title: US-Iran talks in doubt after Trump scraps Pakistan visit
events.description: International efforts to mediate between the United States and Iran were in the balance on Sunday, after President Donald Trump canceled his envoys' planned trip to Pakistan for talks. Trump said there was no point "sitting around talking about nothing" and dismissed Tehran's negotiating position, but added that Iran had revised its proposal within minutes of his decision.

events.title: Gunfire and blasts rock Mali as attackers hit capital and other cities, residents say

events.title: Anthony Albanese accused of ‘caving to gas companies’ as Labor set to reject new export tax
events.description: David Pocock says prime minister – who is trying to shore up fuel supplies – is parroting industry talking points Follow our Australia news live blog for latest updates Get our breaking news email , free app or daily news podcast Labor is poised to reject a growing push for a new 25% tax on gas exports in next month’s budget, prompting David Pocock to accuse the government of “caving in” to the gas industry. It’s understood the government has elected not to pursue a new tax on gas exports in the budget , prompted in part by the global oil crisis and Anthony Albanese’s diplomatic efforts in shoring up fuel supply from Asian allies by pledging reliable access to liquefied natural gas. Continue reading...
events.ticker_label: 💥 Anthony Albanese accused of ‘caving to gas companies’ as Labor set to reject new export tax

events.title: The week in pictures: Trump evacuated at media dinner, Leo XIV in Africa and robots race in Beijing
events.description: US President Donald Trump was evacuated after shots were fired at the annual White House Correspondents' Dinner in Washington DC, Lebanese journalist Amal Khalil was killed in an Israeli strike, the pope visited inmates at an infamous Equatorial Guinea prison and robots outran humans in a Beijing half marathon. Here is a look back at some of the week’s most striking images.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 The week in pictures: Trump evacuated at media dinner, Leo XIV in Africa and robots race in Beijing

events.title: What we know about the suspect in White House press dinner shooting
events.description: Authorities have identified 31-year-old Cole Thomas Allen as the man accused of opening fire outside the White House Correspondents’ Dinner in Washington, D.C.. Ernie Dosio, a 75-year-old vineyard owner, was hunting yellow-backed duiker, an antelope species, in the central African country of Gabon when the incident occurred last Friday. events.title: Australia news live: Canavan says ‘too much talk of diversity’ in address to Canberra anti-immigration rally – as it happened
events.description: This blog is now closed Get our breaking news email , free app or daily news podcast The health and NDIS minister, Mark Butler , says Queensland will be “answerable to their community” if they don’t sign on to NDIS reforms, with billions of dollars of hospital funding on the line. Every state and territory except for the sunshine state has signed a bilateral agreement with the government for the Thriving Kids program, which is designed to take children under nine with developmental delays or low to moderate autism off the NDIS. Now every state and territory has signed a bilateral agreement with us that details the broad details of the Thriving Kids program … The only state that hasn’t signed yet is Queensland. I tried to make clear again last week that is part of the deal that sees them get additional hospital funding and, frankly, they are answerable to their community if they don’t put the additional supports. events.title: Michael Jordan's 1998 NBA Finals sneakers sell for a record $2.2 million
events.description: In 1998, Michael Jordan laced up a pair of his iconic black and red Air Jordan 13s to bring home a Bulls victory during Game 2 of his final NBA championship — and now they are the most expensive sneakers ever to sell at auction. The game-winning sneakers sold for $2.2 million at Sotheby's in New York on Tuesday, smashing the sneaker auction record of $1.47 million, set in 2021 by a pair of Nike Air Ships that Jordan wore earlier in his career.

events.title: Here's the real reason to turn on airplane mode when you fly
events.description: We all know the routine by heart: "Please ensure your seats are in the upright position, tray tables stowed, window shades are up, laptops are stored in the overhead bins and electronic devices are set to flight mode."

events.title: 'I was up to my waist down a hippo's throat.' He survived, and here's his advice
events.description: Paul Templer was living his best life.

events.title: Relaxed entry rules make it easier than ever to visit this stunning Asian nation
events.description: Due to its remoteness and short summer season, Mongolia has long been a destination overlooked by travelers.

events.title: The most beautiful sections of China's Great Wall
events.description: Having lived in Beijing for almost 12 years, I've had plenty of time to travel widely in China.

events.title: Sign up to our newsletter for a weekly roundup of travel news

events.title: Iran's foreign minister leaves Pakistan, then Trump cancels U.S. delegation's travel
events.description: After arriving on Friday, Iran's Abbas Araghchi has left Islamabad, prompting President Trump to announce that his envoys Steve Witkoff and Jared Kushner will no longer travel there Saturday for peace talks

events.title: Syria: First trial of Assad-era officials opens in Damascus
events.description: A court in Damascus is trying one official in former President Bashar Assad's government in person, and several more in absentia. Assad and his brother are among the defendants. Both are thought to have fled to Russia.

events.title: India news: Raghav Chadha, 6 other AAP MPs join BJP
events.description: The exit of seven Aam Aadmi Party MPs has dented the party's strength in the upper house of parliament, sparking fears of political instability. DW has more.

events.title: AI is already getting boring
events.description: It could end white-collar work. It could end poverty. It could end humanity. From AI's boosters and doomers alike, bumptious predictions abound. But in workplaces, the technology, like so many before it, is becoming mundane. Are things really different this time?

events.title: Chernobyl 40th anniversary: A zone that must be sheltered
events.description: Emmanuelle Chaze is live from Ukraine.

events.title: A look at previous attempts on President Trump's life
events.description: FRANCE 24 looks back at previous attempts on US President Donald Trump's life since July 2024.

events.title: Two former Israeli prime ministers agree to merge parties against Netanyahu
events.description: Two Israeli political heavyweights plan to join forces in upcoming elections.

events.title: Syria’s first public trial of Assad-era officials opens in Damascus
events.description: Syria has opened its first public trial of officials linked to former President Bashar Assad

events.title: Panama thanks Cuba for releasing 3 of its citizens as 7 others remain detained
events.description: Panama’s government says Cuba has released three of 10 of its citizens detained on the island earlier this year and accused of fabricating subversive propaganda

events.title: Leaders of France and Greece say the EU's defense splurge is no alternative to the NATO alliance
events.description: The president of France says the European Union’s ongoing push to bolster its own defensive capabilities isn’t intended to spawn an alternative to the NATO alliance but to answer a long-standing U.S. call for the continent to take charge of its own sec...

events.title: Tunisia suspends one of Africa’s oldest rights group as crackdown widens
events.description: Authorities in Tunisia have ordered a one-month suspension of the Tunisian League for Human Rights

events.title: A race for rights: How sport is helping protect girls in Uganda
events.description: On a red running track in eastern Uganda, coach Zuena Cheptoek is doing more than training runners. For many girls in the Sebei subregion, she is also a confidante, a mentor and first line of protection against female genital mutilation, child marriage and abuse.

events.title: For every generation, vaccines work and they have saved over 150 million lives: WHO
events.description: Over the past 50 years, vaccines have saved more than 150 million lives, as ordinary people chose to protect themselves, their children and their communities from diseases like measles, diphtheria, pertussis, polio, and rotavirus.

events.title: Time to apply the brakes to runaway AI, says pioneer
events.description: If AI is “a very fast car with no steering wheel” then regulation must provide one, insists Nobel laureate and Artificial Intelligence pioneer Geoffrey Hinton, the visionary scientist widely known as the “godfather” of the self-learning tech.

events.title: Titan submersible widow says remains of husband and son came back in what looked like small 'shoeboxes'
events.description: Christine Dawood says remains of her husband and son came back in two small boxes nine months after the catastrophic Titan submersible implosion.

events.title: Suspect who fled US arrested in caught-on-camera killing of 15-year-old in NYC park
events.description: Zahir Davis, 18, was charged with murder after allegedly gunning down 15-year-old Jaden Pierre at a Queens playground, according to NYPD officials.

events.title: CBP joins forces with Coast Guard to intercept 3 smuggling boats, apprehend 60 people off California coast
events.description: CBP apprehended 60 people off the California coast during April joint operations with the Coast Guard, intercepting three separate overcrowded vessels.

events.title: Emails reveal reality TV crew’s behind-the-scenes access to sheriff now leading Nancy Guthrie probe
events.description: Emails obtained reveal Pima County Sheriff's Department cooperated extensively with reality TV crew, sharing bodycam footage of arrests and incidents.

events.title: Grinning suspect cuffed after alleged fiery street takeover, drag race caught on camera
events.description: The Queens DA announced the arrest of Asil Assaidi, who allegedly helped wreak havoc during a drag racing street takeover involving more than 100 vehicles.

events.title: Latin American leftists met in Spain, signaling push against US influence on continent
events.description: Analysts say the Barcelona summit led by Spain's Pedro Sánchez signals a progressive bloc forming to challenge U.S. influence in Latin America and beyond.

events.title: US targets Chinese refinery in sweeping Iran oil crackdown, sanctions ‘shadow fleet’ tankers
events.description: The OFAC sanctioned a major Chinese oil refinery and dozens of Iran-linked shadow fleet vessels in an escalation of a maximum pressure campaign on Tehran.

events.title: US economic chokehold on Iran reaches peak leverage as collapse risks emerge
events.description: U.S. economic pressure on Iran has reached historic levels, but inconsistent enforcement limits full impact of sanctions, a former Treasury expert says.

events.title: Iran escalates Hormuz 'tit-for-tat,' seizes ship tied to billionaire close to Trump, Macron
events.description: Analysts say the IRGC seized two vessels in the Strait of Hormuz in a tit-for-tat move after a U.S. naval blockade and seizure of an Iranian ship.
events.ticker_label: 💥 Iran escalates Hormuz 'tit-for-tat,' seizes ship tied to billionaire close to Trump, Macron

events.title: Ex-Philippine president Duterte to face trial on crimes against humanity charges
events.description: The ICC confirmed crimes against humanity charges against former Philippine President Rodrigo Duterte for allegedly overseeing deadly drug crackdowns.

events.title: Trump envoy to Turkey doubles down after backlash, pushes ‘peace through strength’ policy
events.description: U.S. Ambassador to Turkey and Syria envoy Tom Barrack defends controversial remarks on Hezbollah, Turkey’s F-35 access and Middle East governance as pragmatic realism, not policy shifts.
